fields.description:
All BGP e2e jobs are failing with the following error in the `route_advertisements` test suite:

```
fail [github.com/openshift/origin/test/extended/networking/route_advertisements.go:1215]:
Timed out after 180.001s.
Failed to verify external response: failed to execute curl command:
Error running oc --namespace=e2e-bgp-6486 --kubeconfig=/tmp/secret/kubeconfig exec bgp-prober-podbjgcz
-- curl -m 3 -s 172.20.0.100:8000/dial?protocol=http&host=203.131.1.4&port=8000&request=clientip:
StdOut>
StdErr>
curl: option -m 3: expected a proper numerical parameter
curl: try 'curl --help' or 'curl --manual' for more information
command terminated with exit code 2
exit status 2
Expected
    <bool>: false
to be true
```

**Root cause:** The curl invocation passes `-m 3` as two separate arguments (`-m` and `3`). The curl version in the prober image does not accept this form and requires either `-m3` (no space) or `--max-time 3`. This is a test tooling / prober image issue, unrelated to the CNO change under review.

**Affected jobs:**

* `ci/prow/e2e-metal-ipi-ovn-dualstack-bgp`
* `ci/prow/e2e-metal-ipi-ovn-dualstack-bgp-local-gw`
* `ci/prow/e2e-metal-ipi-ovn-dualstack-bgp-local-gw-techpreview` → <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://prow.ci.openshift.org/view/gs/test-platform-results/pr-logs/pull/openshift_cluster-network-operator/2952/pull-ci-openshift-cluster-network-operator-master-e2e-metal-ipi-ovn-dualstack-bgp-local-gw-techpreview/2039734040482811904</custom> 

**Discovered in:** [#2952](https://github.com/openshift/cluster-network-operator/pull/2952)

fields.description:
**Description of problem:**

Note: This case covers multiple issues. This summary for this issue we started to troubleshoot at #120, all data and comments before #120 are irrelevant.

We have two clusters for this customer 

Hub Cluster == Main bare metal cluster  

Hosted Cluster == HCP Cluster running as VM's on the bare metal cluster

Problem: The hosted Cluster is trying to make contact with the hub cluster kube-apiservers (hosted on the masters via the default kubernetes service IP of the hub cluster 

kubevirt-csi ->  172.30.0.1 -> kubernetes service in default namespace HUB cluster -> actual master node ip. 

172.30.0.1 but is unable to communicate normally when contacting the default service we should get a response: see my test cluster response

sh-5.1# nc -zv 172.30.0.1 443  
Ncat: Version 7.92 ( <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://nmap.org/ncat</custom>  )  
Ncat: Connected to 172.30.0.1:443.  
Ncat: 0 bytes sent, 0 bytes received in 0.03 seconds.  
timed out waiting for input: auto-logout

but on the customers hosted cluster when we run the same nc command it throws a TIMEOUT error. 

Iv’e captured TCPDumps of the expected interaction in the comment below and what the 3 master nodes on the hub cluster are seeing. It looks like the traffic is getting mangled and as a result OVS/OVN does not know what to do with it. The key thing were looking for is why and how this happened. I suspect something in OVN/OVS is causing the packets to be mangled 

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

4.20.15

**How reproducible:**

Was consistent 

**Steps to Reproduce:**  
N/A

**Actual results:**

The hosted cluster could no longer communicate via the k8s svc ip to the hub cluster which was required by the csi driver to attach PVC’s to the vm’s provisioned on the hosted cluster

**Expected results:**

Communication should flow normally as seen in test cluster

**Additional info:**

Note: There was an issue on the hub cluster where two master nodes crashed and did not come up after pushing configuration changes, customer had to manually rebuild all the bonds by hand on two nodes n1 and n3 to recover the cluster. n2 was healthy and came up on it's own. Cluster was working fine before the changes were pushed but all 3 master nodes are now seeing this issue

Due to the customers timeline this cluster was destroyed. I gathered as much data as possible before the as customer would like our best effort to find a RCA as they have other simliar environments they work with. Because of this no new data can be collected if asked for. I have sosreports from all 3 master nodes of the hub clusters + the hosted node where the tcpdump was captured, tcpdumps of the failed traffic from hosted to hub via the k8s ip, and must-gathers of both the hub and hosted cluster.

**Description of problem:**

```
Packets are dropping when using EgressIP on VSphere with vmxnet3 driver
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
4.19.19
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Any time in the customer's cluster
```

‌

```
1. Start toolbox in the cluster
2. Start curl each 5 seconds to the remote IP (endpoint)
3. We can see intermittent failures such as rc=28 in the curl and outgoing packets do not reach the remote server
```

```
Intermittent drops when using EgressIP
```

fields.description:
Customer description of the issue:

```java
After loadbalancer went down, the SPK pods went into a Terminating state which is expected. However, the annotation did not get updated to reroute traffic away from the broken ICNI IP address. After pod went into a terminating state, we expected the ICNI to reduce to 3 instead of 4, but it remained at 4. We need to identify what went wrong from an OVN perspective to not detect and update the external gateway to route traffic away from the broken pod. Must-gathers and inspects of OVN and the SPK namespace are in progress. Please let me know what other information would be valuable. 

$ oc describe ns artntnbivzwcsmf-y-ec-conss-001 
Name:         artntnbivzwcsmf-y-ec-conss-001
Labels:       dns-spk=enabled
              istio-discovery=enabled
              istio-injection=enabled
              kubernetes.io/metadata.name=artntnbivzwcsmf-y-ec-conss-001
              namespace=artntnbivzwcsmf-y-ec-conss-001
              pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit=privileged
              pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit-version=v1.24
              pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn=privileged
              pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn-version=v1.24
Annotations:  k8s.ovn.org/external-gw-pod-ips:
                198.19.16.110,198.19.16.111,198.19.16.112,198.19.16.113,fdf0:1049:fe35::6:0:10,fdf0:1049:fe35::6:0:11,fdf0:1049:fe35::6:0:e,fdf0:1049:fe35...
              openshift.io/sa.scc.mcs: s0:c32,c19
              openshift.io/sa.scc.supplemental-groups: 1001030000/10000
              openshift.io/sa.scc.uid-range: 1001030000/10000
Status:       Active

[dietsja@webscale-tools ~]$  oc get pod -n spk-artntnbivzwcsmf-y-ec-conss-001 
NAME                                                            READY   STATUS        RESTARTS   AGE
f5-tmm-545d587655-2bn69                                         6/6     Running       0          84d
f5-tmm-545d587655-85dts                                         0/6     Pending       0          3h16m
f5-tmm-545d587655-czz5d                                         6/6     Running       0          84d
f5-tmm-545d587655-vpfxh                                         6/6     Running       0          84d
f5-tmm-545d587655-zsxtf                                         6/6     Terminating   0          84d
otel-collector-5b76df56b-sq77m                                  1/1     Running       0          84d
spk-artntnbivzwcsmf-y-ec-conss-001-f5ingress-7ffff9b499-6txkr   2/2     Running       0          35d
```

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```java
OpenShift v4.12
```

How reproducible:

```java
- Any time a cluster node goes down
```

Steps to Reproduce:

```java
1. Forcefully power down a cluster node
2. Observe gateway pod in Terminating
3. Observe annotation still contains the pod ip and application traffic still being routed
```

Actual results:

```java
- Gateway pod ip stays in the annotation
- Application traffic still routed to the IP of the pod in Terminating state
```

Expected results:

```java
Help needed to define the expected behavior
```

Additional info:  
In support after our analysis we constructed the following, we need engineering comments on it. Or simply we need engineering to define the expected outcome in this scenario.

```java
When a pod is "completely" deleted following course of actions take place:
- A `DELETE` event is generated by the Kubernetes informer system
- This `DELETE` event is processed in the factory's [handler.go](https://github.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/blob/master/go-controller/pkg/factory/handler.go) through the `DeleteFunc` handler which is registered with the Kubernetes informer.
- This eventually leads to calling `deleteLogicalPort` in [pods.go](https://github.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/blob/master/go-controller/pkg/ovn/pods.go), which is the primary function responsible for cleaning up pod resources in OVN-Kubernetes.
- The `deleteLogicalPort` function is called with the fully deleted pod object, and one of its first actions is to call `deletePodExternalGW(pod)` to clean up any external gateway routes.
- The `deletePodExternalGW` function in [egressgw.go](https://github.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/blob/master/go-controller/pkg/ovn/egressgw.go) checks if the pod had the routing namespace annotation (k8s.ovn.org/routing-namespaces) and if so, it calls `deletePodGWRoutesForNamespace` for each namespace.
- The `deletePodGWRoutesForNamespace` function:
  - Removes the pod from the namespace's routing external pod gateways list
  - Deletes the actual gateway routes in OVN
  - Updates the `k8s.ovn.org/external-gw-pod-ips` annotation on the namespace to remove this pod's IP
  
In your case the node went down and kubelet set the deletion timestamp which changed the node status to Terminating. And the apiserver could not be updated by kubelet in time to completely remove the pod from etcd. In this case ovn controller receives an `UPDATE` event not a `DELETE` event. As explained above the `DELETE` event handler is connected to removal of pod resources in ovn controller. `DELETE` event is generated only after you forcefully deleted the pod. A similar situation could have happened if the pod had a finalizer for instance. Of course in that case the containers would stay up and there would be no disruption in the traffic.
```

Affected Platforms:

```java
Baremetal OpenShift Cluster
```

fields.description:
Description of problem:

Cluster config: ROKS 4.20 with OVN default CNI.

Observation: UDN attached CNV VMs loose their IP address and DHCP availability during cluster master operation (tested with patch version update).

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

ROKS 4.20.12.

CNV 4.20.3

How reproducible: 100%

Steps to Reproduce:

0\. ROKS cluster with master version 4.20.12.

1\. Create a namespace with primary UDN label. In the example, the namespace name is \`green\`

2\. Create a layer2 UDN with the following NAD:  
\`\`\`

kind: NetworkAttachmentDefinition

  config: '{"allowPersistentIPs":true,"cniVersion":"1.0.0","joinSubnet":"100.65.0.0/16,fd99::/64","name":"cluster_udn_green-net","netAttachDefName":"green/green-net","role":"primary","subnets":"10.203.0.0/26","topology":"layer2","type":"ovn-k8s-cni-overlay"}'  
\`\`\`

3\. Install CNV 4.20.3 and create some Centos VMs in the \`green\` namespace in such a way that it is attached to the UDN. Verify VM is getting the IP from the UDN over DHCP without any issue. 

\`\`\`

$ k get vmi -A                                              
NAMESPACE   NAME       AGE   PHASE     IP            NODENAME                                                READY

green       example2   69m   Running   10.203.0.13   test-d6is4ks20q37r7i27big-gergo03022-default-0000036a   True  
green       example3   61m   Running   10.203.0.44   test-d6is4ks20q37r7i27big-gergo03022-default-00000244   True  
green       example4   58m   Running   10.203.0.46   test-d6is4ks20q37r7i27big-gergo03022-default-0000036a   True  
green       example5   57m   Running   10.203.0.47   test-d6is4ks20q37r7i27big-gergo03022-default-0000036a   True

\`\`\`

4\. Update the master to a new patch level (in this case the target level is 4.20.13.). Wait until DHCP lease expires in the Centos VMs (probably 30 minutes).

5\. Check the attached IPs again.

\`\`\`

$ k get vmi -A                                                 
NAMESPACE   NAME       AGE    PHASE     IP                      NODENAME                                                READY  
green       example1   141m   Running                           test-d6is4ks20q37r7i27big-gergo03022-default-00000244   True  
green       example2   141m   Running   fe80::858:aff:fecb:d    test-d6is4ks20q37r7i27big-gergo03022-default-0000036a   True  
green       example3   133m   Running                           test-d6is4ks20q37r7i27big-gergo03022-default-00000244   True  
green       example4   129m   Running   fe80::858:aff:fecb:2e   test-d6is4ks20q37r7i27big-gergo03022-default-0000036a   True  
green       example5   129m   Running   fe80::858:aff:fecb:2f   test-d6is4ks20q37r7i27big-gergo03022-default-0000036a   True

\`\`\`

Actual results: VMs loose their DHCP assigned IPv4 addresses and only link-local v6 addresses are present. VM logs shows unavailable DHCP service.

Expected results: DHCP is not disrupted.

Additional info:

I am not sure if this issue is specific to this master patch update. It might be that this is just one way to trigger it.

VM guest OS restart does not recover the issue. Any DHCP client restart also does not recover.

Given the VMI is owned by a VM object, deleting the VMI is resolving the issue, as new VMI comes up.

Attaching (the same) static IP on the guest instead using DHCP client also works, suggesting the datapath in OVS is generally available, maybe only DHCP is affected. Pods attached to the same UDN are also not disrupted, as they do not use DHCP.

Affected Platforms:

Is it an issue present and reproducible with any IBM Cloud managed Openshift (ROKS) cluster with OVN as the default CNI.

fields.summary: Bootstrap deadlock due to ClusterResourceQuota Admission plugin

components.name: Cluster Version Operator
components.description: Top level operator that coordinates OpenShift upgrades. Bugs in specific operators that are blocking upgrades should be filed against their respective component.

projectCategory.description: Hybrid Platform ‒ OCP Area ‒ Org-Wide Shared Projects
projectCategory.name: HP ‒ OCP ‒ Org-Wide Shared

fields.description:
Since <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/pull/1266</custom>, cluster bootstrapping is routinely broken on ABI installs (\~90% failure rate, see e.g. [here](https://prow.ci.openshift.org/job-history/gs/test-platform-results/pr-logs/directory/pull-ci-openshift-installer-main-e2e-agent-compact-ipv4), [here](https://prow.ci.openshift.org/job-history/gs/test-platform-results/pr-logs/directory/pull-ci-openshift-assisted-service-master-e2e-agent-compact-ipv4?buildId=2039561359162609664), & [here](https://prow.ci.openshift.org/job-history/gs/test-platform-results/pr-logs/directory/pull-ci-openshift-assisted-installer-master-e2e-agent-compact-ipv4?buildId=2039561359162609664)).

The liveness check on the bootstrap kube-apiserver is failing with the error:

```
[-]poststarthook/rbac/bootstrap-roles failed: reason withheld
```

The reason is that the [ClusterResourceQuota CRD](https://github.com/openshift/api/blob/2757d677e9aaac40a9985e5a6a17b31c7c577ba8/payload-manifests/crds/0000_03_config-operator_01_clusterresourcequotas.crd.yaml#L4) must be applied in the first 30s after startup (the timeout of the rbac/bootstrap-roles post-start hook). This is a problem because of a dependency in kube-apiserver:

1. ClusterResourceQuota admission plugin tries to sync informers on startup
2. Informer LIST fails because CRD doesn't exist yet
3. Plugin blocks ALL namespaced resource requests for 10s waiting for sync
4. ServiceAccounts get created BEFORE the CRD (alphabetical ordering)
5. Requests blocked → 10s timeout → failures → liveness check fails → pod  
  killed

 <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/commit/90fd30316ae61cded9785ea969e575b7b155803a</custom> (merged March 26) added ServiceAccounts (`0000_00_cluster-version-operator_02_service_account-*.yaml`) that are the first namespaced resources in bootstrap for a namespace that actually exists, and they come _before_ `0000_03_config-operator_01_clusterresourcequotas.crd.yaml`. Therefore there is a longer delay before the CRD is applied and this causes the liveness check to restart the pod before that can occur.

Originally there were 2 namespaced resources causing 10s delays prior to the ClusterResourceQuota CRD:

* [0000_00_cluster-version-operator_03_deployment.yaml](https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/blob/f0070658e2cc847726f6a98f4f4d176d882d2782/install/0000_00_cluster-version-operator_03_deployment.yaml)
* [0000_00_cluster-version-operator_04_service.yaml](https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/blob/f0070658e2cc847726f6a98f4f4d176d882d2782/install/0000_00_cluster-version-operator_04_service.yaml)

Other namespaced resources don’t cause delays because they fail for other reasons:

* [0000_00_cluster-version-operator_01_adminack_configmap.yaml](https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/blob/90fd30316ae61cded9785ea969e575b7b155803a/install/0000_00_cluster-version-operator_01_adminack_configmap.yaml) fails because the `openshift-config` namespace does not exist yet
* [0000_00_cluster-version-operator_01_admingate_configmap.yaml](https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/blob/90fd30316ae61cded9785ea969e575b7b155803a/install/0000_00_cluster-version-operator_01_admingate_configmap.yaml) fails because the `openshift-config-managed` namespace does not exist yet
* [0000_00_cluster-version-operator_02_networkpolicy.yaml](https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/blob/f0070658e2cc847726f6a98f4f4d176d882d2782/install/0000_00_cluster-version-operator_02_networkpolicy.yaml) doesn’t appear to be deployed at this time… except in <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-2">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-81187</custom> for some reason. It later fails with `unable to get REST mapping for "0000_00_cluster-version-operator_02_networkpolicy.yaml": no matches for kind "NetworkPolicy" in version "networking.k8s.io/v1"`. 

Now there are 4:

* [0000_00_cluster-version-operator_02_service_account-cluster-version-operator.yaml](https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/blob/90fd30316ae61cded9785ea969e575b7b155803a/install/0000_00_cluster-version-operator_02_service_account-cluster-version-operator.yaml)
* [0000_00_cluster-version-operator_02_service_account-update-payload.yaml](https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/blob/90fd30316ae61cded9785ea969e575b7b155803a/install/0000_00_cluster-version-operator_02_service_account-update-payload.yaml)
* [0000_00_cluster-version-operator_30_deployment.yaml](https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/blob/90fd30316ae61cded9785ea969e575b7b155803a/install/0000_00_cluster-version-operator_30_deployment.yaml) (pod killed while applying this)
* [0000_00_cluster-version-operator_40_service.yaml](https://github.com/openshift/cluster-version-operator/blob/90fd30316ae61cded9785ea969e575b7b155803a/install/0000_00_cluster-version-operator_40_service.yaml)

This guarantees 40s of dead time before the CRD is applied. I guess if we get lucky and circle back to that resource at the exact time the apiserver pogos up again then we might succeed, and this probably accounts for the few jobs that are green.

fields.summary: Prevent Scaling of Machine Config Operator Components to Zero Replicas in `openshift-machine-config-operator` Namespace to Avoid Configuration Drift and Inconsistencies

fields.description:
**Description of problem:**

```
In the openshift-machine-config-operator namespace, critical control plane components such as the Machine Config Controller can currently be manually scaled down to zero replicas by a cluster administrator. This behavior introduces a risk of configuration drift and inconsistent node states.
For example, if the Machine Config Controller is scaled down to zero and a new MachineConfig (MC) is applied, the system does not have an active controller to reconcile the change. This can lead to delayed or missed configuration propagation to nodes, potentially leaving the cluster in a partially updated or unstable state.
At present, there are no built-in safeguards or warnings to prevent scaling these critical deployments to zero, nor mechanisms to ensure reconciliation continuity during such scenarios.
```

fields.description:
**Description of problem:**

```
The is requesting to implement a non-standard GC script in the MachineConfig
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
4.19.17
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Not Implemented Yet
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

‌

‌

```
The customer wants to implement a MC using standard and documented features from OCP to enable the GC of the nodes. They do not want to use KubeletConfig parameters to implement the GC because from their prespective it can generate several impacts in the cluster. Because of that they want to implement the MC for node-level cleanup.

From the GSS perspective we need to confirm if their MC is supportable and if will not have impacts in the overall timers of the cluster (mainly kubelet and crio)

1. MachineConfig for deploying files and systemd units — This is a documented and supported use case. MachineConfig is the standard mechanism for deploying custom systemd services, scripts, and configuration files to CoreOS nodes. Our MachineConfig does exactly this — it deploys a script and a systemd timer. It does not modify any OpenShift or Kubernetes configuration parameters.

Reference: https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/openshift_container_platform/4.20/html/installation_configuration/installing-customizing

2. crictl rm on exited containers — Your team has already confirmed this is a supported mitigation tool (KCS 5610941). Our script only runs crictl rm on containers that are already in Exited state and older than a defined age threshold.

3. systemd timers — These are a standard CoreOS/RHEL feature. Deploying custom systemd timers via MachineConfig is a common and documented pattern (e.g., custom log rotation, node monitoring scripts).

```

**Actual results:**

```
 The customer has about 17K Exited pods to be wiped and the standard GC is not working due to the threshold:

 --terminated-pod-gc-threshold int32     Default: 12500
	
Number of terminated pods that can exist before the terminated pod garbage collector starts deleting terminated pods. If <= 0, the terminated pod garbage collector is disabled.
```

**Expected results:**

```
Customer wants to implement MC to wipe the dead Containers
```

**Additional info:**

```
Hi Fabio,


Thank you for the response. We would like to clarify a misunderstanding about our approach.


We are NOT running crictl rm during the MachineConfig rollout. The MachineConfig only deploys two files to each worker node:



A bash script 

A systemd timer (container-gc.timer)


After the one-time rollout (standard reboot like any MC change), the systemd timer runs the script every 10 minutes independently. No further reboots, no further rollouts, no MachineConfig involvement during cleanup.


The script only removes exited containers that exceed a threshold (e.g., 5000) and are older than 1 hour. It does not remove all containers. In our testing, removing 600 excess containers took ~46 seconds with no impact on the node.


Both components are individually supported:



MachineConfig for deploying systemd units and files (documented in OCP installation customization guide)

crictl rm on exited containers (supported as mitigation per KCS 5610941)


Please find the MachineConfig YAML and the script attached for your review.


container-gc.sh (deployed to /usr/local/bin/ via MachineConfig):


#!/bin/bash
MAX_DEAD_CONTAINERS=5000
MIN_AGE_SECONDS=3600

logger -t container-gc "Starting container garbage collection check"

DEAD_IDS=$(crictl ps -a --state Exited -q 2>/dev/null)
DEAD_COUNT=$(echo "$DEAD_IDS" | grep -c . 2>/dev/null || echo 0)

logger -t container-gc "Current dead containers: $DEAD_COUNT (threshold: $MAX_DEAD_CONTAINERS)"

if [ "$DEAD_COUNT" -le "$MAX_DEAD_CONTAINERS" ]; then
  logger -t container-gc "Below threshold, no action needed"
  exit 0
fi

TO_REMOVE=$((DEAD_COUNT - MAX_DEAD_CONTAINERS))
logger -t container-gc "Need to remove $TO_REMOVE containers"

REMOVED=0
for CID in $DEAD_IDS; do
  if [ "$REMOVED" -ge "$TO_REMOVE" ]; then break; fi

  FINISHED=$(crictl inspect "$CID" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '"finishedAt":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
  if [ -z "$FINISHED" ]; then
    crictl rm "$CID" >/dev/null 2>&1 && REMOVED=$((REMOVED + 1))
    continue
  fi

  FINISHED_EPOCH=$(date -d "$FINISHED" +%s 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -z "$FINISHED_EPOCH" ]; then
    crictl rm "$CID" >/dev/null 2>&1 && REMOVED=$((REMOVED + 1))
    continue
  fi

  NOW_EPOCH=$(date +%s)
  AGE=$((NOW_EPOCH - FINISHED_EPOCH))

  if [ "$AGE" -ge "$MIN_AGE_SECONDS" ]; then
    crictl rm "$CID" >/dev/null 2>&1 && REMOVED=$((REMOVED + 1))
  fi
done

logger -t container-gc "Removed $REMOVED dead containers. Remaining: $((DEAD_COUNT - REMOVED))"
MachineConfig YAML (machineconfig-container-gc.yaml):


apiVersion: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1
kind: MachineConfig
metadata:
  labels:
    machineconfiguration.openshift.io/role: worker
  name: 99-worker-container-gc
spec:
  config:
    ignition:
      version: 3.2.0
    storage:
      files:
        - path: /usr/local/bin/container-gc.sh
          mode: 0755
          overwrite: true
          contents:
            source: data:text/plain;charset=utf-8;base64,<BASE64_ENCODED_SCRIPT>
    systemd:
      units:
        - name: container-gc.service
          enabled: false
          contents: |
            [Unit]
            Description=Container Garbage Collector
            Wants=crio.service
            After=crio.service
            [Service]
            Type=oneshot
            ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/container-gc.sh
            TimeoutStartSec=300
            [Install]
            WantedBy=multi-user.target
        - name: container-gc.timer
          enabled: true
          contents: |
            [Unit]
            Description=Run container GC every 10 minutes
            [Timer]
            OnBootSec=5min
            OnUnitActiveSec=10min
            AccuracySec=1min
            Persistent=true
            [Install]
            WantedBy=timers.target
Given that there is no supported count-based container GC, KubeletConfig eviction does not address our scenario (no resource pressure) — could you please reconsider whether this approach can be used?


Thanks

```

fields.summary: Our customer has reported the following issues to Red Hat Support that they have encountered after a CNI migration from OpenShiftSDN to OVNKubernetes:

components.name: Networking / ovn-kubernetes
components.description: Bugs related to the ovn based openshift networking plugin

projectCategory.description: Hybrid Platform ‒ OCP Area ‒ Org-Wide Shared Projects
projectCategory.name: HP ‒ OCP ‒ Org-Wide Shared

fields.description:
Case 04393258  
Problem: For the namespace in which egress is assigned, after the migration internal traffic for namespace (internal to namespace and internal to cluster) is not working. When they remove the egress IP and add it back, connectivity starts working as expected. However, the problem resurfaces after working for some time. Customer has to remove and reattach the EgressIPs to make the connectivity work again. Clusters currently running 4.16.30 and 4.16.36.

Case 04390281  
Problem: Vault and external connectivity for the customer broke after the SDN to OVN upgrade for their cluster namicggtd30d. They mentioned that SMCP is present for this project. They discovered that the root cause was not EgressIP, but rather how the services were binding. Specifically, the pods could not bind to the same port simultaneously; when one pod bound to a NodePort, the second pod would fail. The RH Support Engineer resolved this by creating an additional service and exposing a separate NodePort. According to Citi, while it remains unclear why this functioned with a single port under SDN but requires two under OVN, they feel that SDN and OVN typically should not affect port bindings other than the default port changes used for encapsulation (e.g., VXLAN vs. Geneve). They want to know the key differences between SDN and OVN and what is causing these inherent issues when they migrate. They want a comprehensive document about pre-steps and post-steps they can take with the OVN migration to prevent these issues from occurring

Case 04387488  
Problem: Application was down and returned the error 'This site can't be reached' after the migration. Also, the application's vanity URL and other routes were not working. Upon checking, they noticed the network policy "allow-from-openshift-ingress" was missing in the namespace. Once they added back the network policy 'allow-from-openshift-ingress' to the project, the application started to work properly. They need to understand that how the network policy 'allow-from-openshift-ingress' got deleted. please check and advise.

Case 04393258  
Problem: After migration from SDN to OVN on Cluster namicggtd51d, client is not able to access a specific URL from inside the Pod in their namespace, which was working fine before the migration. One thing they noticed is that the issue gets fixed after they remove and add back the Egress IP assigned to the Namespace. But after sometime the issue happens again and they have to reattach the EgressIPs for the connectivity to work again (possibly related to Case 04393258.

We are not sure if any of this is related to the bugs that were fixed with version 4.16.53. The customer will upgrade to this version if Red Hat requires them to. However, considering that they have 600+ clusters to upgrade, they want to be sure that the upgrade will indeed fix their issues as it is not a trivial task for them to do the upgrades on such a large farm of clusters. If some of these issues are not related to the fixes made in version 4.16.53, they would like to know what other steps they need to take so these issues do not surface following future migrations.

fields.summary: MCO cleanup removes locally created initramfs-etc tracking added via a custom service, which causes NBDE Tang decryption to fail on reboot in DHCP-less environments.

components.name: Installer / Agent based installation
components.description: First cluster deployment using "openshift agent" subcommand. It generates bootable ephemeral images that run agent based installation on the machines that are to become cluster nodes.

components.name: Machine Config Operator
components.description: The MCO manages and applies configuration and updates of the base operating system and container runtime; essentially everything between the kernel and kubelet.

components.name: RHCOS
components.description: Red Hat CoreOS (RHCOS) is the immutable host used by OpenShift to run containers.

projectCategory.description: Hybrid Platform ‒ OCP Area ‒ Org-Wide Shared Projects
projectCategory.name: HP ‒ OCP ‒ Org-Wide Shared

fields.description:
In DHCP-less setups using the Agent-Based Installer (ABI) with Network-Bound Disk Encryption (NBDE), static network configurations are not automatically included in the initramfs of the target system. Since early boot requires network access to the Tang server for disk decryption, administrators must manually ensure that NetworkManager and DNS configurations are tracked:

```
rpm-ostree initramfs-etc --track /etc/NetworkManager --track /etc/resolv.conf
```

To automate this, the customer implemented a MachineConfig that deploys a one-time systemd service (`initramfs-track.service`) to run this command during the first boot.

**The Issue:**  
The service runs successfully on the first boot and creates a new local rpm-ostree deployment containing the required network configuration. However, this deployment is not recognized by the Machine Config Operator (MCO) as part of the desired system state.

When the MCO later performs its routine cleanup, it removes this locally created deployment. As a result, the initramfs network configuration is lost without any explicit changes or upgrades. On the next reboot, the node cannot reach the Tang server and fails to decrypt the disks.

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1.  Deploy an OpenShift cluster using ABI with NBDE in a DHCP-less environment (static IPs defined in `agent-config.yaml`). 
2. Apply a MachineConfig that installs a one-shot systemd service to run:

    ```
    rpm-ostree initramfs-etc --track /etc/NetworkManager --track /etc/resolv.conf
    ```

    on first boot.  
      
    `cat /etc/systemd/system/initramfs-track.service`



    `[Unit]`



    `Description=Generate initramfs overlay via rpm-ostree`



    `After=local-fs.target`



    `Before=multi-user.target`



    



    `[Service]`



    `Type=oneshot`



    `ExecStart=/usr/bin/rpm-ostree initramfs-etc --track /etc/NetworkManager --track /etc/resolv.conf`



    `ExecStartPost=/bin/systemctl disable initramfs-track.service`



    `RemainAfterExit=yes`



    



    `[Install]`



    `WantedBy=multi-user.target`


3.  Confirm that a new deployment is staged and the overlay is present under `/boot/ostree/`. 
4.  Allow the node to continue running until the MCO performs a cleanup (or trigger it via a MachineConfig update). 
5.  Run `rpm-ostree status -v` on the node. 

**Actual Results:**

*  The tracked files are no longer present in the rpm-ostree state. 
*  The overlay directory under `/boot/ostree/` is removed. 
* System logs show MCO-triggered cleanup removing the deployment:

    ```
    rpm-ostree[...] Initiated txn Cleanup for client(id:machine-config-operator ...)
    ```
*  After reboot, the node fails to decrypt disks due to missing network configuration required to reach the Tang server. 

**Expected Results:**

* Changes introduced via `rpm-ostree initramfs-etc --track` should persist across MCO reconciliation and cleanup operations.

fields.summary: Dual-stack cluster: Node with link-local IPv6 prevents new nodes from joining and destabilizes OVN-Kubernetes

components.name: Networking / ovn-kubernetes
components.description: Bugs related to the ovn based openshift networking plugin

projectCategory.description: Hybrid Platform ‒ OCP Area ‒ Org-Wide Shared Projects
projectCategory.name: HP ‒ OCP ‒ Org-Wide Shared

fields.description:
**Description of problem:**

When adding new nodes to a dual-stack cluster, if an existing node has an invalid IPv6 configuration (link-local only), it blocks newly added nodes from becoming Ready.

Additionally, this leads to instability in OVN-Kubernetes, where ovnkube-node pods on control-plane nodes enter CrashLoopBackOff.

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

OpenShift Version: 4.x

**How reproducible:**

**Steps to Reproduce:**  
1.Add a node with IPv6 configured as link-local only in a dual-stack (IPv4/IPv6) cluster  
2.Approve the node certificates → node remains NotReady with warning:

```
k8s.ovn.org/node-chassis-id annotation not found
```

3\. Add another node with correct network configuration and approve certificates

**Actual results:**

* Newly added node remains in NotReady state
* Healthy node provisioning is blocked until the faulty node is fixed
* ovnkube-node pods on control-plane nodes enter CrashLoopBackOff
* Cluster networking becomes unstable

**Expected results:**

* Node with invalid IPv6 configuration should remain NotReady
* Newly added nodes with correct configuration should become Ready
* ovnkube-node pods should remain stable (no crashes)
* Faulty node should not impact other nodes joining the cluster

**Additional info:**

* Once the faulty node’s IP configuration is corrected, all nodes transition to Ready
* Observed CrashLoopBackOff in ovnkube-controller container on master nodes
* Sample issue seen in pod:

```
Reason: CrashLoopBackOff
failed to run ovnkube
```

* Node states confirm dependency on fixing the faulty node before others recover

---

Please fill in the following template while reporting a bug and provide as much relevant information as possible.    
Doing so will give us the best chance to find a prompt resolution.

---

### Affected Platforms

  (especially if it's a telco use case like ICNI, secondary bridges or BM + kubevirt).

---

### If it is a CI failure

* Did it happen in different CI lanes? If so, please provide links to multiple failures with the same error instance.  
* Did it happen in both SDN and OVN jobs? If so, please provide links to multiple failures with the same error instance.  
* Did it happen on other platforms (e.g. AWS, Azure, GCP, Bare Metal)? If so, please provide links to multiple failures with the same error instance.  
* When did the failure start happening? Please provide the **UTC timestamp** of the networking outage window from a sample failure run.  
* If it's a connectivity issue, please provide:

    * srcNode, srcIP, srcNamespace, and srcPodName  
    * dstNode, dstIP, dstNamespace, and dstPodName  
    * traffic path (examples: pod→pod, pod→external, pod→svc, pod→node, etc.)
    

---

### If it is a Customer / SD issue

* Provide enough information in the bug description that Engineering doesn’t need to read the entire case history.  
* Don’t presume that Engineering has access to Salesforce.  
* Do presume that Engineering will access attachments through supportshell.  
* Describe what each relevant attachment is intended to demonstrate (failed pods, log errors, OVS issues, etc.).  
* Referring to the attached must-gather, sosreport or other attachment, please provide the following details:

* dstNode, dstNamespace, dstPodName, dstPodIP

#### If it is **not** a connectivity issue:

* Describe the steps taken so far to analyze the logs from networking components (cluster-network-operator, OVNK, SDN, openvswitch, ovs-configure, etc.)  
* Indicate where the issue was observed based on the attached must-gather.  
* Attach relevant log snippets around the window when the problem occurred (if any).

---

### Additional Guidance

* When showing results from commands, include the **entire command** in the output.  
* For OCPBUGS in which the issue has been identified → label with `sbr-triaged`  
* For OCPBUGS in which the issue has not been identified and needs Engineering help → label with `sbr-untriaged`  
* **Do not** set the priority — that is owned by Engineering and will be set during evaluation.  
* Note: bugs that do not meet these minimum standards will be closed with label `SDN-Jira-template`

fields.description:
Two issues have been observed with some of the (C)UDN service flows 

* At least one UDN service flow relies on the ClusterIP field of the service instead of the ClusterIPs field

    ```
    flows := []string{fmt.Sprintf("cookie=%s, priority=300, table=2, %s, %s_dst=%s, "+
    				"actions=set_field:%s->eth_dst,output:%s",
    				defaultOpenFlowCookie, ipPrefix, ipPrefix, service.Spec.ClusterIP,
    				npw.ofm.getDefaultBridgeMAC().String(), defaultNetConfig.ofPortPatch)}
    ```
* At least one UDN service flow hardcodes a field on IPv4 family

    ```
    flows = append(flows, fmt.Sprintf("cookie=%s, priority=490, in_port=%s, ip, ip_src=%s,actions=ct(zone=%d,nat,table=3)",
    					defaultOpenFlowCookie, defaultNetConfig.ofPortPatch, service.Spec.ClusterIP, config.Default.HostMasqConntrackZone))
    ```

fields.description:
This is a clone of issue <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-73788#icft=OCPBUGS-73788</custom>. The following is the description of the original issue:   
—  
Two issues have been observed with some of the (C)UDN service flows 

* At least one UDN service flow relies on the ClusterIP field of the service instead of the ClusterIPs field

    ```
    flows := []string{fmt.Sprintf("cookie=%s, priority=300, table=2, %s, %s_dst=%s, "+
    				"actions=set_field:%s->eth_dst,output:%s",
    				defaultOpenFlowCookie, ipPrefix, ipPrefix, service.Spec.ClusterIP,
    				npw.ofm.getDefaultBridgeMAC().String(), defaultNetConfig.ofPortPatch)}
    ```
* At least one UDN service flow hardcodes a field on IPv4 family

    ```
    flows = append(flows, fmt.Sprintf("cookie=%s, priority=490, in_port=%s, ip, ip_src=%s,actions=ct(zone=%d,nat,table=3)",
    					defaultOpenFlowCookie, defaultNetConfig.ofPortPatch, service.Spec.ClusterIP, config.Default.HostMasqConntrackZone))
    ```

For the first issue, no actual consequence has been observed although it is expected that traffic flows to the non-preferred IP family of the KAPI/DNS services (so IPv6 on dual stack) will fail.

The second issue has been observed on a single stack IPv6 cluster causing the complete flow sync to fail permanently when a CUDN is advertised, making traffic flows CUDN<->external to fail (and potentially impacting any further networking change afterwards):

fields.summary: OpenShift Installer Does Not Set Explicit maxUnavailable in MachineConfigPools

components.name: Installer / openshift-installer
components.description: Problems related to using the openshift installer binary to install a cluster.

components.name: Machine Config Operator
components.description: The MCO manages and applies configuration and updates of the base operating system and container runtime; essentially everything between the kernel and kubelet.

projectCategory.description: Hybrid Platform ‒ OCP Area ‒ Org-Wide Shared Projects
projectCategory.name: HP ‒ OCP ‒ Org-Wide Shared

fields.description:
**Description of problem:**

```
OpenShift installer creates MachineConfigPools (both master and worker) without explicitly setting the spec.maxUnavailable field, relying on implicit defaults. This causes Red Hat Insights to flag fresh out-of-the-box (OOTB) clusters with Risk Level 2 warnings, creating confusion for administrators who expect OOTB configurations to comply with Red Hat's own best practices.
                                                                                                                                                                                              
The implicit default (maxUnavailable: 1) is functionally correct, especially for master pools with etcd quorum requirements, but the lack of explicit configuration:                        
- Triggers false-positive Insights recommendations (rule: CCX_RULES_OCP.EXTERNAL.RULES.MCP_UNAVAILABLE_MACHINE_COUNT)
- Creates operational visibility gaps (admins cannot see actual behavior without reading documentation)                                                                                     
- Contradicts the principle of explicit configuration for safety-critical parameters                                                                                                        
- Causes confusion: "Why is Red Hat warning about Red Hat's own defaults?"                                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                                                                                              
This is a product quality issue affecting all IPI-installed clusters. 
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
OpenShift Container Platform 4.20.14, 4.20.15                                                                                                                                               
Likely affects all 4.x versions
```

**How reproducible:**

```
100% - Verified on multiple clusters across different OpenShift versions
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
  1. Install fresh OpenShift 4.20.x cluster using IPI method on AWS                                                                                                                           
  2. Check MachineConfigPool configuration:
     oc get machineconfigpool master worker -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, maxUnavailable: .spec.maxUnavailable}'                                                            
  3. Verify both master and worker MCPs show "maxUnavailable: null"                                                                                                                           
  4. Wait for cluster nodes to reboot (or wait for scheduled Insights data collection)                                                                                                        
  5. Check Insights recommendations:                                                                                                                                                          
     oc get insightsoperator cluster -o json | jq '.status.insightsReport.healthChecks[] | select(.description | contains("MachineConfigPool"))' 
```

**Actual results:**

````
  Installer creates MachineConfigPools with maxUnavailable field omitted:                                                                                                                     
   
  ```yaml                                                                                                                                                                                     
  apiVersion: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1                                                                                                                                          
  kind: MachineConfigPool                                                                                                                                                                     
  metadata:                                                                                                                                                                                   
    name: master                                                                                                                                                                              
  spec:                                                                                                                                                                                       
    # maxUnavailable field omitted - defaults to 1 implicitly                                                                                                                                 
    nodeSelector:                                                                                                                                                                             
      matchLabels:                                                                                                                                                                            
        node-role.kubernetes.io/master: ""
  ```
  
  Result:                                                                                                                                                                                     
  - maxUnavailable: null (field not set)                                                                                                                                                      
  - Insights flags this with warning: "MachineConfigPool will never finish updating when unavailableMachineCount > maxUnavailable"                                                            
  - Risk Level: 2 (Medium)                                                                                                                                                                  
  - Recommendation appears inconsistently after node reboots or data collection events                                                                                                        
  - Creates operational confusion on OOTB clusters   
````

**Expected results:**

```
  Installer should create MachineConfigPools with explicit maxUnavailable configuration:                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  apiVersion: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1                                                                                                                                            
  kind: MachineConfigPool                                                                                                                                                                     
  metadata:                                                                                                                                                                                   
    name: master                                                                                                                                                                              
  spec:                                                                                                                                                                                       
    maxUnavailable: 1  # EXPLICIT value for safety                                                                                                                                            
    nodeSelector:                                                                                                                                                                             
      matchLabels:                                                                                                                                                                            
        node-role.kubernetes.io/master: ""                                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  Expected behavior:                                                                                                                                                                          
  - maxUnavailable: 1 explicitly set during cluster bootstrapping                                                                                                                             
  - No Insights warnings on OOTB clusters                                                                                                                                                     
  - Clear visibility into MCP update behavior without consulting documentation                                                                                                              
  - Consistent with Red Hat best practices for explicit configuration  
```

**Additional info:**

```
  Evidence from Production Clusters:                                                                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                            
  Cluster 1 (api.myocp.sandbox3491.opentlc.com):                                                                                                                                              
  - OpenShift: 4.20.14                                                                                                                                                                      
  - Installed: 2026-03-22                                                                                                                                                                     
  - Master MCP: maxUnavailable: null                                                                                                                                                        
  - Worker MCP: maxUnavailable: null                                                                                                                                                          
  - Insights alert: Present (appeared 2026-03-31 after node reboots)                                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  Cluster 2 (api.nreg.sandbox2845.opentlc.com):                                                                                                                                               
  - OpenShift: 4.20.15                                                                                                                                                                        
  - Installed: 2026-03-29                                                                                                                                                                     
  - Master MCP: maxUnavailable: null                                                                                                                                                          
  - Worker MCP: maxUnavailable: null                                                                                                                                                          
  - Insights alert: Not yet present (expected on next data collection)                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  Why This Matters for Master Pools:                                                                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  All 3 master machines have EtcdQuorumOperator preDrain lifecycle hooks:                                                                                                                     
  oc get machine -n openshift-machine-api -o json | \                                                                                                                                         
    jq '.items[] | select(.metadata.name | contains("master")) | .spec.lifecycleHooks'                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  These hooks require maxUnavailable: 1 to prevent etcd quorum loss during updates. The implicit default is correct, but should be explicit for:                                              
  - Safety-critical parameter visibility                                                                                                                                                      
  - Operational transparency                                                                                                                                                                  
  - Compliance with Red Hat's own tooling recommendations                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  Insights Rule Details:                                                                                                                                                                      
  - Rule ID: ccx_rules_ocp.external.rules.mcp_unavailable_machine_count                                                                                                                       
  - Description: "MachineConfigPool will never finish updating when the 'unavailableMachineCount' is greater than 'maxUnavailable' in the MachineConfigPool"                                  
  - Total Risk: 2 (Medium)                                                                                                                                                                    
  - Advisory URL: https://console.redhat.com/openshift/insights/advisor/clusters/{cluster-id}?first=ccx_rules_ocp.external.rules.mcp_unavailable_machine_count
```

fields.summary: Component Readiness: [Machine Config Operator] [Other]  PinnedImages GC test image cleanup issue

components.name: Machine Config Operator
components.description: The MCO manages and applies configuration and updates of the base operating system and container runtime; essentially everything between the kernel and kubelet.

projectCategory.description: Hybrid Platform ‒ OCP Area ‒ Org-Wide Shared Projects
projectCategory.name: HP ‒ OCP ‒ Org-Wide Shared

fields.description:
\[Generated AI Summary\]                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
1\. Test creates a custom MachineConfigPool                                                                                                                                                                         

The PinnedImages test (\[sig-mco\]\[OCPFeatureGate:PinnedImages\] All Nodes in a custom Pool should have the PinnedImages even after Garbage Collection) moves a worker node into a custom MCP. This causes MCO to       
render a new config for that pool — hence rendered-custom-<hash>.                                                                                                                                                  

2\. Worker node references the custom rendered config                                                                                                                                                               

The affected node (always one of the workers — worker-b, worker-a, etc.) gets assigned rendered-custom-<hash> as its desired MachineConfig while in the custom pool.                                               

3\. Test cleanup deletes the custom MCP — but the node still references the old config                                                                                                                              

When the test cleans up and deletes the custom MachineConfigPool, the rendered-custom-<hash> MachineConfig object is also removed. However, the node still has an annotation referencing that rendered config. The   
node's MCD (Machine Config Daemon) then tries to reconcile against a config that no longer exists.

4\. Node gets stuck in a degraded state                                                                                                                                                                             

From the verify operator conditions machine-config error:                                                                                                                                                          

MachineConfigPool worker is not ready                                                                                                                                                                                
total: 3, ready: 2, updated: 2, unavailable: 1                                                                                                                                                                     
Node worker-b reporting: "machineconfig rendered-custom-<hash> not found"                                                                                                                                        

The node can't complete its config sync because the target config doesn't exist. It:                                                                                                                               

* Reports upgrade failure                                                                                                                                                                                          
* Stays NotReady / Unschedulable                                                                                                                                                                                   
* The worker MCP shows 1 unavailable node
* MCO operator goes Degraded (RequiredPoolsFailed)                                                                                                                                                                 

‌                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
5\. The PinnedImages test itself also times out                                                                                                                                                                     

Timed out after 300.003s.                                                                                                                                                                                            
Timed out waiting for Node 'worker-b' to have rendered-worker config.                                                                                                                                              

This confirms the node never transitions back to rendered-worker config after leaving the custom pool. The 300s timeout expires and the node remains stuck.                                                        

Why this is intermittent (4 of 7 recent runs)                                                                                                                                                                      

The pass sequence SSFSFFFSSSSS suggests a timing/race condition — the cleanup sometimes works correctly (node transitions back to rendered-worker) and sometimes doesn't. Possible factors:                        

* Race between MCP deletion and node config reconciliation — if the rendered config is deleted before the node's MCD picks up the new target, it gets stuck
* Order of operations during pool removal — the node needs to be moved back to the worker pool and get rendered-worker as its desired config before the custom pool's rendered config is garbage collected         
* Recent code change around March 27-28 may have introduced or worsened this race, given 25 consecutive passes before that date                                                                           

‌                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
In short                                                                                                                                                                                                           

The worker is NotReady because it's trying to apply a MachineConfig that was deleted when the custom MachineConfigPool was cleaned up. The MCO never successfully transitions the node back to the standard          
rendered-worker config, leaving it permanently stuck until the job ends.

—  
Description of problem:

```java
The catalog-operator Pod crashed during Single Node OpenShift (SNO) cluster install, seemingly due to the bundles for the operators failing. Rather than continuing and entering the "bundle unpacking" error in the operators' Subscriptions, the catalog-operator Pod crashed instead:

---

time="2026-02-11T15:44:22Z" level=info msg="unpacking bundles" id=h6EhF namespace=metallb-system
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x38 pc=0x1f94bf0]

goroutine 528 [running]:
github.com/operator-framework/operator-lifecycle-manager/pkg/controller/bundle.sortUnpackJobs.func1(0xc005573ef0?, 0x0)
        /build/vendor/github.com/operator-framework/operator-lifecycle-manager/pkg/controller/bundle/bundle_unpacker.go:889 +0x1f0
sort.insertionSort_func({0xc0059b3090?, 0xc004d39a80?}, 0x0, 0x5)
```

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```java
4.18.24
```

How reproducible:

```java
Inconsistent, appears to require some variation of the Jobs failing
```

Steps to Reproduce:

```java
1. Install 4.18.24 SNO cluster
    2. Attempt to install multiple operators
    3. Have the bundles of some operators fail
```

Actual results:

```java
catalog-operator Pod crashes
```

Expected results:

```java
catalog-operator Pod continues, labeling each Subscription as having an issue with their bundles
```

Additional info:

* Issue appears to be similar but separate to <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-2">https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-34173#icft=OCPBUGS-34173</custom>
* Issue is still resolved using the [solution for the bundle unpacking error](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/6459071)

fields.summary: ClusterExtension webhook-operator install stalls in RollingOut because the generated deployment never becomes Available

components.name: OLM
components.description: Operator Lifecycle Manager (OLM) installs and manages the upgrade and lifecycle of cluster extensions.

projectCategory.description: Hybrid Platform ‒ OCP Area ‒ Org-Wide Shared Projects
projectCategory.name: HP ‒ OCP ‒ Org-Wide Shared

fields.description:
**Description of problem:**

```
ClusterExtension installation can stall during rollout and fail to reach Progressing=True, Reason=Succeeded within the expected time. In the failing scenario, the install enters RollingOut but does not complete because one of the managed resources does not become available. This causes the installation to time out before the extension is reported as successfully installed.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
4.22
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Intermittent in QE case 83979
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. Create a namespace and admin service account for the install.
2. Create a ClusterCatalog from image quay.io/olmqe/webhook-operator-index:0.0.3-v1-cache and wait for Serving=True.
3. Create a ClusterExtension for package webhook-operator, channel alpha, version 0.0.1, targeting the created namespace.
4. Wait for ClusterExtension Progressing reason to become Succeeded.
```

**Actual results:**

```
ClusterExtension does not reach Progressing=True, Reason=Succeeded within 150 seconds.

Observed condition progression from the provided log:

11:28:02: Progressing reason = Retrying
11:28:08: Progressing reason = RollingOut
11:28:08 through 11:30:29: remains RollingOut
QE fails with:
error: clusterextension Progressing expected is not Succeeded in 150 seconds
```

**Expected results:**

```
The ClusterExtension installation should complete successfully for the supported webhook operator flow:

Progressing=True, Reason=Succeeded
Installed=True
Webhook operator deployment becomes Available=True
```

**Additional info:**

```
QE case:
[sig-olmv1][Jira:OLM] clusterextension PolarionID:83979-[OTP][Skipped:Disconnected]ClusterExtension installs webhook-operator and webhooks work
```

* Possible root cause area:  
  rollout/orchestration for webhook operator installs in `operator-controller`, specifically the path that waits for the generated webhook deployment to become available.

fields.summary: [release-4.18] Annotations: k8s.ovn.org/external-gw-pod-ips is not being updated when gateway pod goes down

fields.description:
Customer description of the issue:

```java
After loadbalancer went down, the SPK pods went into a Terminating state which is expected. However, the annotation did not get updated to reroute traffic away from the broken ICNI IP address. After pod went into a terminating state, we expected the ICNI to reduce to 3 instead of 4, but it remained at 4. We need to identify what went wrong from an OVN perspective to not detect and update the external gateway to route traffic away from the broken pod. Must-gathers and inspects of OVN and the SPK namespace are in progress. Please let me know what other information would be valuable. 

$ oc describe ns artntnbivzwcsmf-y-ec-conss-001 
Name:         artntnbivzwcsmf-y-ec-conss-001
Labels:       dns-spk=enabled
              istio-discovery=enabled
              istio-injection=enabled
              kubernetes.io/metadata.name=artntnbivzwcsmf-y-ec-conss-001
              namespace=artntnbivzwcsmf-y-ec-conss-001
              pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit=privileged
              pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit-version=v1.24
              pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn=privileged
              pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn-version=v1.24
Annotations:  k8s.ovn.org/external-gw-pod-ips:
                198.19.16.110,198.19.16.111,198.19.16.112,198.19.16.113,fdf0:1049:fe35::6:0:10,fdf0:1049:fe35::6:0:11,fdf0:1049:fe35::6:0:e,fdf0:1049:fe35...
              openshift.io/sa.scc.mcs: s0:c32,c19
              openshift.io/sa.scc.supplemental-groups: 1001030000/10000
              openshift.io/sa.scc.uid-range: 1001030000/10000
Status:       Active

[dietsja@webscale-tools ~]$  oc get pod -n spk-artntnbivzwcsmf-y-ec-conss-001 
NAME                                                            READY   STATUS        RESTARTS   AGE
f5-tmm-545d587655-2bn69                                         6/6     Running       0          84d
f5-tmm-545d587655-85dts                                         0/6     Pending       0          3h16m
f5-tmm-545d587655-czz5d                                         6/6     Running       0          84d
f5-tmm-545d587655-vpfxh                                         6/6     Running       0          84d
f5-tmm-545d587655-zsxtf                                         6/6     Terminating   0          84d
otel-collector-5b76df56b-sq77m                                  1/1     Running       0          84d
spk-artntnbivzwcsmf-y-ec-conss-001-f5ingress-7ffff9b499-6txkr   2/2     Running       0          35d
```

Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):

```java
OpenShift v4.12
```

How reproducible:

```java
- Any time a cluster node goes down
```

Steps to Reproduce:

```java
1. Forcefully power down a cluster node
2. Observe gateway pod in Terminating
3. Observe annotation still contains the pod ip and application traffic still being routed
```

Actual results:

```java
- Gateway pod ip stays in the annotation
- Application traffic still routed to the IP of the pod in Terminating state
```

Expected results:

```java
Help needed to define the expected behavior
```

Additional info:  
In support after our analysis we constructed the following, we need engineering comments on it. Or simply we need engineering to define the expected outcome in this scenario.

```java
When a pod is "completely" deleted following course of actions take place:
- A `DELETE` event is generated by the Kubernetes informer system
- This `DELETE` event is processed in the factory's [handler.go](https://github.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/blob/master/go-controller/pkg/factory/handler.go) through the `DeleteFunc` handler which is registered with the Kubernetes informer.
- This eventually leads to calling `deleteLogicalPort` in [pods.go](https://github.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/blob/master/go-controller/pkg/ovn/pods.go), which is the primary function responsible for cleaning up pod resources in OVN-Kubernetes.
- The `deleteLogicalPort` function is called with the fully deleted pod object, and one of its first actions is to call `deletePodExternalGW(pod)` to clean up any external gateway routes.
- The `deletePodExternalGW` function in [egressgw.go](https://github.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/blob/master/go-controller/pkg/ovn/egressgw.go) checks if the pod had the routing namespace annotation (k8s.ovn.org/routing-namespaces) and if so, it calls `deletePodGWRoutesForNamespace` for each namespace.
- The `deletePodGWRoutesForNamespace` function:
  - Removes the pod from the namespace's routing external pod gateways list
  - Deletes the actual gateway routes in OVN
  - Updates the `k8s.ovn.org/external-gw-pod-ips` annotation on the namespace to remove this pod's IP
  
In your case the node went down and kubelet set the deletion timestamp which changed the node status to Terminating. And the apiserver could not be updated by kubelet in time to completely remove the pod from etcd. In this case ovn controller receives an `UPDATE` event not a `DELETE` event. As explained above the `DELETE` event handler is connected to removal of pod resources in ovn controller. `DELETE` event is generated only after you forcefully deleted the pod. A similar situation could have happened if the pod had a finalizer for instance. Of course in that case the containers would stay up and there would be no disruption in the traffic.
```

Affected Platforms:

```java
Baremetal OpenShift Cluster
```

In <=4.20, there exists a [machine count conditional](https://github.com/openshift/machine-config-operator/blob/53228343a48901cbdcc3a159c5c77e4e47e2ca13/pkg/controller/node/status.go#L210-L224) that has caused some confusion in the MCN GA and bug fixing efforts. While it's accuracy and purpose has been questioned in a few occasions, the conditional has remained due to its historical performance and to keep the scope of changes appropriately sized. This bug should aim to update the main \`calculateStatus\` function to be clearer.

Note that the \`calculateStatus\` function was refactored slightly in 4.21, so this bug only pertains to <=4.20.

Please fill in the following template while reporting a bug and provide as much relevant information as possible.

### Affected Platforms

Is it an:

### If it is an internal Red Hat testing failure

* Did it happen in both SDN and OVN jobs? If so, please provide links to multiple failures with the same error instance.

    * dstNode, dstIP, dstNamespace, and dstPodName  
    * traffic path (examples: pod→pod, pod→external, pod→svc, pod→node, etc.)
    

---

### If it is a Customer / SD issue

* dstNode, dstNamespace, dstPodName, dstPodIP

* Indicate where the issue was observed based on the attached must-gather.  
* Attach relevant log snippets around the window when the problem occurred (if any).

---

* When showing results from commands, include the **entire command** in the output.

fields.summary: Set Ceph noout Flag During SDN-to-OVN Migration to Prevent Unnecessary Recovery

components.name: Networking / openshift-sdn
components.description: Issues related to the core openshift sdn

components.name: Networking / ovn-kubernetes
components.description: Bugs related to the ovn based openshift networking plugin

fields.description:
**Description of problem:**

```
During the planned SDN-to-OVN migration, a rolling reboot of all nodes will be performed. This can trigger unnecessary Ceph recovery operations if not handled properly.

As highlighted in internal communication, clusters running ODF must have the Ceph noout flag set before starting the migration and unset only after all nodes have successfully rejoined and Ceph reports a healthy status.

This step is critical to avoid unnecessary data rebalancing and recovery during node restarts.

Steps to Perform:

Before starting SDN-to-OVN migration, execute:

ceph osd set noout
Proceed with rolling migration/reboots.

After all nodes are back online and cluster status is HEALTH_OK, execute:

ceph osd unset noout
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
‌
```

**How reproducible:**

```
Do not set noout flag before migration for a cluster with ODF.
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**

```
1. Ensure a cluster is deployed with ODF (Ceph) and is in healthy state (HEALTH_OK).
2. Do not set the Ceph noout flag.
3. Initiate SDN-to-OVN migration which triggers a rolling reboot of nodes.
4. Observe the cluster during node restarts:
   - Nodes go down one by one.
   - Ceph detects OSDs as temporarily unavailable.

5. Check Ceph cluster status during the process:
   - ceph -s
6. Observe that Ceph begins recovery/backfilling/rebalancing operations due to perceived OSD failures.
7. Continue until all nodes reboot and rejoin the cluster.
```

**Actual results:**

```
Unstable cluster after the node restart.
```

**Expected results:**

```
No unnecessary Ceph recovery/rebalancing during migration.
Stable cluster behavior throughout node restarts.
```

**Additional info:**

```
KCS: 6495631 (Ceph noout usage guidance)
Support Case: 04373200
```

fields.summary: 4.20 - Extreme CPU Usage observed from Kubernetes components due to golang-fips TLS1.3 handling

components.name: kube-apiserver
components.description: Kube API Server, Kube API Server Operator, Admission and Quota for OpenShift v4 (include all Kubernetes APIs)

components.name: Release
components.description: Release Engineering tasks

projectCategory.description: Hybrid Platform ‒ OCP Area ‒ Org-Wide Shared Projects
projectCategory.name: HP ‒ OCP ‒ Org-Wide Shared

fields.description:
**Description of problem:**

```
Customer is seeing huge CPU usage on the kube-apiserver after upgrading to OCP 4.20.

Customer managed to get a pprof from an API server as this occurred 
and found that 60%+ of the time was being spent handling TLS handshakes with the following call tree:

runtime.cgocall                                    164.63s  61.05%
  crypto/tls.(*Conn).HandshakeContext              161.88s  60.03%
    crypto/tls.(*serverHandshakeStateTLS13).handshake  148.86s  55.20%
      crypto/tls.expandLabel                       154.47s  57.28%
        io.ReadAll                                 153.35s  56.86%
          golang-fips/openssl/v2.(*hkdf).Read
            _Cfunc_go_openssl_EVP_PKEY_derive_wrapper 

Customer noticed that compiling the API server using https://github.com/golang-fips/go and 
believes the root cause of the issues is due the expandLabel handling in there, specifically 
this looks to be very expensive: https://github.com/golang-fips/go/blob/f9990dae1ad17df49fe7ec70a707e764fc3c0004/patches/000-fips.patch#L4694-L4699 
when tracing the code due to the number of times expandLabel is called in a handshake and the 
implementation of that between the IO handling and the reader.

This started to happen in 4.20 and customer's assumption is because images were upgraded 
to be compiled with Go 1.24 which has a much different handling of expandLabel in 1.24 vs 1.23

This is causing huge impact on the stability of the cluster.

Customer also sees that this issue is reproducible when shutting down one kube-apiserver,
the same behavior starts on the other kube-apiservers.
```

**Version-Release number of selected component (if applicable):**

```
OpenShift Container Platfrom 4.20.5
With enabled FIPS
```
